title: Giant Coral Discovered in Pacific Ocean
story: Imagine a coral so big it dwarfs a blue whale! On November 13, 2024, scientists made an incredible discovery off the Solomon Islands. title: Voyager 1's Stellar Saturn Snapshots
story: Zooming through space, Voyager 1 made history on November 13, 1980. The plucky spacecraft captured the first close-up pictures of Saturn during its daring fly-by. Scientists back on Earth eagerly awaited each photo, learning more about the mysterious gas giant than ever before. Voyager 1's mission sparked a new era of planetary exploration, inspiring future generations of space enthusiasts.

title: Ancient Winemaking Secrets Uncovered
story: Archaeologists made a grape discovery in 2017! They unearthed evidence of the world's oldest winemaking in Khramis Didi Gora, Georgia. On November 13, researchers found ancient clay pots dating back to 6,000 BC. These pots held the key to understanding how our ancestors first created wine. It's amazing to think that people were enjoying grape juice's bubbly cousin over 8,000 years ago!
activity_question: Where was the world's earliest winemaking discovered?
bonus_fact: The ancient winemakers used clay pots called qvevri, which are still used in Georgian winemaking today.

title: Snowmaking Magic on Mount Greylock

title: Big Ben's First Chime
story: Bong! A deep, resonant sound echoed across London in 1856. The Great Bell of Big Ben rang out for the first time on November 13. title: Underwater Tunnel Connects Two States
story: Cars zoomed beneath the Hudson River for the first time in 1927. The Holland Tunnel opened on November 13, connecting New York and New Jersey. This amazing underwater passageway was the first of its kind for automobiles. It had two separate tubes, one for each direction of traffic. Engineers solved tricky problems to keep the air clean for drivers in this groundbreaking tunnel.

bonus_fact: The tunnel was named after its chief engineer, Clifford Holland.

title: Powderfinger's Final Farewell
story: Fans cheered as Powderfinger rocked the stage one last time. The beloved Australian band played their farewell concert on November 13, 2010, at the Brisbane River Stage. After years of hit songs and electrifying performances, they decided to end on a high note. The crowd sang along to every tune, creating an unforgettable night of music and memories.
activity_question: Where did Powderfinger perform their last concert?
follow_up_question: Why do you think bands sometimes decide to stop performing together?

title: Baseball's Brightest Stars Shine
story: Baseball fans cheered as two incredible players claimed the top honors in 2014. Clayton Kershaw, a pitcher for the Los Angeles Dodgers, and Mike Trout, an outfielder for the Los Angeles Angels, both won the Most Valuable Player (MVP) award on November 13. It was a rare double win for players from the same area!
activity_question: Which two players won the MVP award in 2014?
bonus_fact: MVP stands for Most Valuable Player, the highest individual honor in baseball.

title: Iconic Miami Beach Hotel Crumbles to Dust
story: Boom! The famous Deauville Hotel in Miami Beach vanished in a cloud of dust. Built in 1957, this swanky spot once welcomed big names like The Beatles and Frank Sinatra. Sadly, it closed in 2017 after an electrical fire. On November 13, 2022, the old hotel met its end in a controlled implosion, marking the end of an era for Florida's glamorous beachfront.
activity_question: Which famous musical group once stayed at the Deauville Hotel?
bonus_fact: Controlled implosions use carefully placed explosives to make buildings collapse inward, minimizing damage to surroundings.
